This approach is now deprecated. ```tsx import { NotFoundRoute, createRouter } from '@tanstack/react-router' import { Route as rootRoute } from './routes/__root' import { routeTree } from './routeTree.gen' const notFoundRoute = new NotFoundRoute({ getParentRoute: () => rootRoute, component: () =>

Not found!!!

This context will be available to all routes. ```typescript import { createRouter } from '@tanstack/react-router' import { routeTree } from './routeTree.gen' const router = createRouter({ routeTree, context: { foo: true, }, }) ``` ### Handling Errors with routeOptions.errorComponent Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/guide/data-loading.md The `routeOptions.errorComponent` option allows you to specify a custom component that will be rendered when an error occurs during the route loading or rendering lifecycle. This component receives `error` and `reset` props. The `error` prop contains the error that occurred, and the `reset` function can be used to retry rendering the error boundary's children.

### Create TanStack Router Instance Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/framework/react/examples/kitchen-sink Configure and create a router instance with route tree, default pending and error components, context, and scroll restoration. ```typescript const router = createRouter({ routeTree, defaultPendingComponent: () => (
), defaultErrorComponent: ({ error }) => , context: { auth: undefined!, }, defaultPreload: 'intent', scrollRestoration: true, }) declare module '@tanstack/react-router' { interface Register { router: typeof router } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Creating the Router Instance Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/framework/react/examples/kitchen-sink Example of creating the main router instance with the defined route tree and a default pending component. ```javascript const router = createRouter({ routeTree, defaultPendingComponent: () => ( ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure 404 Not Found Route Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/guide/creating-a-router.md Add a `notFoundComponent` to `createRootRoute` to specify the UI to render when no other route matches. ```tsx export const Route = createRootRoute({ component: () => ( // ... ), notFoundComponent: () =>
404 Not Found
, }); ``` -------------------------------- ### DefaultGlobalNotFound Component Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/api/router/defaultGlobalNotFoundComponent.md Renders 'Not Found' on the root route when no other route matches and a notFoundComponent is not provided. This is the default behavior. ```tsx

Not Found

``` ```tsx

Not Found

``` ### Using the default ErrorComponent Source: https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/guide/data-loading.md TanStack Router includes a default `ErrorComponent` that serves as a fallback for any uncaught errors during route loading or rendering. Even when overriding route-specific error components, it is recommended to include a fallback to this default `ErrorComponent` to ensure all errors are handled gracefully.

state.title: src/routes/__root.tsx

parts.text:
Here's the plan:

**Changes to `src/router.tsx`:**

1. Import `LoadingPage`, `ErrorPage`, and `NotFoundPage` from `@/components/pages/`
2. Add three router options to the `createRouter` call:
   - `defaultPendingComponent: () => <LoadingPage />`
   - `defaultErrorComponent: ({ error, reset }) => <ErrorPage error={error} reset={reset} />`
   - `defaultNotFoundComponent: () => <NotFoundPage />`

The `ErrorPage` component already accepts `{ error, reset }` which matches TanStack Router's `defaultErrorComponent` signature. The other two are simple wrappers.

**Note:** `defaultNotFoundComponent` is the router-level fallback. The root route (`__root.tsx`) doesn't currently have a `notFoundComponent`, so adding it at the router level is the right approach. If you later want route-specific 404 handling (e.g., nested not-found within a layout), you'd add `notFoundComponent` to individual routes.

No other files need changes. Want me to proceed?
The user wants me to proceed with the changes. Let me edit `src/router.tsx` to add the component pages.
